sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 2.52 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.2)

statement.latex: (F.\,Bruhat, N.\,Iwahori, M.\,Matsumoto). Let$G$be a semisimple algebraic group over a locally compact, totally disconnected field. Do the maximal compact subgroups of$G$fall into finitely many conjugacy classes? If so, estimate this number. { V.\,P.\,Platonov} Yes, they do, and a formula for this number is found (F.\,Bruhat, J.\,Tits, Publ. Math. IHES, { 41} (1972), 5--251).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
